personal_info.name: Arunachalam Manikandan
personal_info.title: CS Grad Student at University of Minnesota, Twin Cities | AI Engineer @ Supercell | GenAI Intern @ LyftBridge Innovation | Former SDE Intern @Visa Inc '23

about.summary: I design and deploy scalable LLM architectures, RAG pipelines, and agentic frameworks that make cutting-edge AI usable, responsive, and production-ready. I also work across the stack — building robust APIs, backend systems, and occasionally web or mobile interfaces, with a strong foundation in machine learning and real-world deployment.
about.education_research: I'm a Computer Science graduate student at the University of Minnesota, Twin Cities, focused on AI, Machine Learning, NLP, and Advanced Software Engineering. I serve as a Graduate Research Assistant in the Epidemiology department, helping model HPV-related cancer outcomes, and as a Teaching Assistant at the Carlson School of Management, guiding students in Big Data technologies like Spark and AWS.
about.current_work: Currently, I'm working on multiple cutting-edge AI projects: at LyftBridge Innovation, I'm integrating ElevenLabs real-time conversational agents with Google Cloud Run APIs to automate Gmail, Docs, and Calendar workflows, achieving 70% reduced response latency. At SmartPAL AI, I'm architecting unified serverless systems with AWS Lambda and LangGraph to orchestrate 30+ ETL jobs, boosting execution speed by 50%.
about.previous_experience: At the AI Innovation Lab @ Supercell, I architected a multi-agent framework with LLaMA 3 and Agentic RAG, delivering real-time reasoning with sub-100ms latency and orchestrating FAISS-based vector memory systems handling 1M+ embeddings. Previously at Visa Inc., I developed large-scale Spark pipelines in Scala, validating 15M+ records daily to ensure data integrity. I completed my undergraduate studies at the College of Engineering, Guindy, diving into AI, OS, databases, and distributed systems.
about.beyond_classroom: I thrive in fast-paced, creative environments and recently won the AI x Journalism Hackathon with MinneDigest, securing $10,000 in funding. I'm passionate about multimodal AI research, having achieved 85.7% accuracy in sarcasm detection using fine-tuned Qwen2.5-VL models. Outside of work, I explore vision-language models, agentic frameworks, and real-time AI systems.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science

education.institution: College of Engineering Guindy, Anna University
education.degree: Bachelor of Technology in Information Technology

responsibilities: Integrated ElevenLabs real-time conversational agents with Google Cloud Run APIs (FastAPI + Zapier MCP) to automate Gmail, Docs, Sheets, and Calendar workflows, reducing response latency by 70% compared to the prior local deployment.
Engineered Cloud Run webhook tools for knowledge retrieval, communication, and scheduling, cutting manual task and deployment time by 40% and enhancing system reliability.

Automated a unified serverless architecture with AWS Lambda, EC2, S3, DynamoDB, FastAPI, and LangGraph to orchestrate 30+ ETL jobs, centralizing configuration in serverless.yml and boosting execution speed by 50%.

Architected a multi-agent framework with LLaMA 3 and Agentic RAG, integrating planning, personality, and emotion-belief systems to deliver real-time reasoning with <100 ms latency across local and cloud endpoints.

Orchestrated a per-agent FAISS-based vector memory system handling 1M+ embeddings within the framework, enabling long-term recall, high-precision contextual retrieval, and cross-agent knowledge sharing.
Assisted in teaching MSBA 6331 – Big Data Analytics at the Carlson School of Management, guiding students through hands-on labs and assignments using Hadoop, Hive, Spark, and AWS.
Designed and graded assignments, homeworks, and lab tasks to reinforce student understanding of scalable data processing and machine learning pipelines.
Proctored exams and provided individualized support to students, bridging conceptual learning with practical implementation of Big Data technologies.
Developed and maintained Java-based simulation models for dual-stain cancer screening, driving research on early detection strategies and patient outcome optimization.
Designed and executed comprehensive simulation tests across large datasets to validate screening model accuracy, adaptability, and reliability.
Built robust JUnit-based testing frameworks and working on CI/CD pipelines to support continuous integration and reproducible research workflows.

Built a scalable, distributed ETL pipeline using Spark and Scala to process 15M+ financial records daily, performing batch validation, cross-table reconciliation, and real-time anomaly detection across Visa's data infrastructure.

Designed automated data quality monitoring and reporting workflows with custom error-handling logic, reducing data integrity issues by 50% and improving reliability of downstream analytics for compliance and business intelligence teams.
Designed and deployed an AI-powered chatbot to streamline the U.S. visa application process, enabling applicants to receive instant answers to visa-related queries.
Implemented natural language understanding using Microsoft LUIS and integrated QnA Maker for dynamic knowledge retrieval to enhance response accuracy and user engagement.
Gained hands-on experience in conversational AI development using enterprise-grade cloud services, dialog flow design, and user-centric chatbot architecture.

projects.name: Multimodal Sarcasm Detection using Vision-Language Models
description: Optimized and fine-tuned Qwen2.5-VL (3B/7B) on a custom multimodal sarcasm dataset using LoRA via Unsloth, applying 4-bit quantization to cut inference latency from 10 to 1 min and achieving 85.7% accuracy with a +6.5% F1 score gain over Gemini 2.0.

projects.name: MinneDigest
description: Integrated an AI-powered news platform that generates multilingual summaries and human-like podcasts, winning the AI x Journalism Hackathon and securing $10,000 in funding for product scaling.

Developed a scalable backend with FastAPI, ChatGPT, and LLaMA 3 (via Ollama) to automate summarization, podcast generation, and translation, cutting content creation time by 85% and enabling white-labeled deployment for local news outlets.
projects.achievement: Won AI x Journalism Hackathon - $10,000 in funding

projects.name: Streamlining Architectural Workflows Using Generative AI
description: Implemented an end-to-end AI system to generate 2D floorplans and photorealistic interior designs from natural language prompts using a hybrid NER-GNN-MLP model, Neural Style Transfer, and CycleGANs, cutting manual design time by 40% while ensuring compliance with TNCDBR 2019 housing regulations and Vastu principles.

Evaluated architectural layout generation performance across multiple LLMs, ChatGPT-3.5, LLaMA3 and Mistral and published results in IEEE CVMI 2024, demonstrating a 70% improvement in spatial layout efficiency and design iteration speed.

cloud_backend_data: AWS (Lambda, DynamoDB, EC2, S3, IAM, SageMaker, VPC)
Google Cloud GCP (Cloud Run, Cloud Build, APIs, Compute Engine, Cloud Storage)
